title: Dwindling inventories pushes copper higher
summary:
Main
Easing trade tensions helped push the energy sector higher. Metals gained on signs of tightness as supply disruptions mount. By Daniel Hynes
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2025-06-06 144439.png | assets/2025-06-06_dwindling-inventories-pushes-copper-higher_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_8a94fedd5b7b.png

Market Commentary
Copper rose to a fresh two month high amid ongoing signs of tightness.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Easing trade tensions helped push the energy sector higher. Metals gained on signs of tightness as supply disruptions mount.

children.title: Market Commentary
children.summary: Copper rose to a fresh two month high amid ongoing signs of tightness. The recent plunge in inventories in London Metal Exchange warehouses continued, with order for another 14kt from facilities in South Korea and Netherlands.

children.title: Chart of the Day
children.summary: The OPEC+ alliance approved a 411kb/d increase in output for July. This will bring the increase in supply to 1.38mb/d in the first four months of the phase out plan, or 64% of the 2.2mb/d in voluntary production cuts.
